label: Ego-graph orientation layer on PC skeleton
grounding_detail: MosaCD [#1], chatPC [#4], CausalFusion [#2], CauScientist [#3] leave local ego-graph middle ground unexplored. V2 proposal narrows claim to orientation-layer operating point.

label: PC-stable skeleton backend (Phase 0)
grounding_detail: PC-stable [Colombo & Maathuis 2014] is order-independent and standard in LLM-causal papers. CPC as robustness variant.

label: Ego-graph prompting as LLM interface (Phase 1)
grounding_detail: Core thesis: ego-graph is richer than per-edge (exposes phi consistency potential) yet bounded (O(d) context). MVE will test empirically. Degree cap at 8.

label: Local hard-constraint compilation / Max-2SAT (Phase 2)
grounding_detail: CI-derived collider/non-collider constraints form 2-SAT clauses. Exact enumeration feasible for d<=8. LLM soft scores as objective. Infeasibility policy pre-specified.

label: Dual-endpoint Dawid-Skene reconciliation (Phase 3)
grounding_detail: Dawid-Skene [1979] for latent-label aggregation. Each edge gets 2+ judgments from endpoint egos. Borrowed module, not a contribution. EBCC sensitivity mandatory.

label: Conservative propagation + Meek closure (Phase 4)
grounding_detail: Meek [1995] rules are sound and complete for CPDAG orientation. Conservative scheduling inspired by decimation literature. Rollback safeguards for early commitments.

label: Budgeted per-edge fallback (Phase 5)
grounding_detail: MosaCD-style per-edge queries reserved for hard tail (~10-15% of edges). Budget-capped to preserve selective output.

label: Venn-Abers calibration + selective output (Phase 6)
grounding_detail: Multiclass Venn-Abers [Vovk et al. 2004, 2025]. Edge-level selective output with calibrated confidence. Formal claims restricted to exchangeable graph instances only.

label: Confirmatory benchmark suite
grounding_detail: Asia, Sachs, Alarm, Insurance (overlap with MosaCD/chatPC). 100-node ER and scale-free synthetic. Oracle + estimated skeletons. Descriptions + anonymized metadata.

label: Orientation-only primary evaluation mode
grounding_detail: V2 scopes primary claim to orientation quality given fixed skeleton. End-to-end is secondary. Per audit C01 recommendation.

label: EBCC/BCC dependence-aware reconciliation
grounding_detail: Endpoint egos share LLM and variable descriptions -> not independent. EBCC [Kim & Ghahramani 2012] handles correlated annotators. Mandatory sensitivity check on confirmatory subset.

label: Hub-degree splitting strategy
grounding_detail: Nodes with degree > 8 need sub-ego splitting. Options: graph clustering, sepset-informed grouping, overlapping sub-egos. Details TBD.

label: 200-node scaling experiments
grounding_detail: Budget-dependent. Feasibility audit estimates $150-400+ for 200-node runs. Appendix only unless budget allows.

label: Real-world large graph (e.g., Neuropathic Pain 221-node)
grounding_detail: Metadata curation is the blocker. Feasibility audit flags as likely blocker for v1. Appendix-only or drop.

label: Metadata leakage control protocol
grounding_detail: Need standardized description protocol with provenance and scrub pass removing causal language. Critical for anonymized condition. Details TBD.

label: LLM-based skeleton refinement (recover PC-missed edges)
rejected: pairwise LLM edge queries for missing edges
rationale: Three independent root causes: (1) most GT-missing edges are >2 hops apart in skeleton (unreachable), (2) LLM too conservative on genuinely ambiguous edges, (3) approach outside proposal scope (proposal says LLM does not alter skeleton). Negative result strengthens F1 decomposition contribution.

label: MosaCD re-implementation for same-model fair comparison
decision: Re-implement MosaCD's 5-step algorithm from paper (no public repo) using same LLM (Qwen3.5-27B) and same skeleton (PC-stable)
rejected: use reported MosaCD numbers directly (different model, different skeleton)
reproduce with GPT-4o-mini (different model confounds comparison)
rationale: Isolates methodological difference (ego-graph+NCO vs per-edge+shuffled-debiasing+propagation) from model difference. Paper provides complete algorithm, all 4 prompt templates, and parsing regex.

label: Iterative BFS ego-graph expansion (SP-style decimation)
decision: Negative result: single-pass K=10 beats multi-round K=5x2 on Insurance (-6.3pp F1), ties on Alarm. More votes > more context.
rationale: Original proposal design from raw_idea.md. Tested but iterative context enrichment does not improve over single-pass. Error amplification from early decimation and fewer votes per round are the root causes.
